# Transcrição: 13-Split Testing

## [00:00 - 03:03]

I want to introduce you now to Facebook split tests. Facebook introduces split test tool a few back and I think in the most part people still don't know that it's there or how and when to use it. Now I use Facebook split tests fairly frequently and here are a few tests that I run to give you an idea of how and when to potentially use it. Now how does a split test tool work? What it does is you give Facebook some variables so it could be an audience or an ad or a pixel event and Facebook will split the traffic to ensure that one person will only ever see one variant of whatever you're testing. So for example if I'm testing an event split test so for this particular campaign all we did was to run the same audience and split the event optimizing either for purchase or initiate checkout so that's the only difference it was exactly the same ad that was running it was the same audience the only difference was a pixel event and the goal there you know this is this is how your testing should start what's the question you want to ask for me I wanted to ask for this date for this audience and for this ad what's the best pixel event to optimize for in my prospecting and scaling so we've gone through testing we've got ads and audiences that works but I wanted to retest the pixel event purchase versus initiate checkout the funnel for this was add to lander to check out check out to purchase so it's a fairly low fairly simple process and we ran this as a split test and the results we got back were actually that there wasn't much difference between the two of them now although the spend wasn't enough to drive sales the spend was enough to give us an indication as to kind of what's happening with the various metrics and those with purchase intent although they were slightly more expensive they were also as likely to hit our initiate checkout page as optimizing for initiate checkout so for this test it was a really simple low-cost test it was more aimed at looking at the metrics to see how they differ across both both pixel events so that's a real crude simple test then I'll give you an example of one where we were actually looking at landing pages so I think it's let me just expand this out so here yeah it's this one here so this one was now looking at we have two landing pages for this particular product which landing page performs better now the advantage of using a split test test in facebook to do this is that

## [03:04 - 06:05]

when when I split the traffic in two so I got um in fact I've got three audiences here when I split the traffic what I wanted to actually do with this particular test so it wasn't testing the old page versus new what I specifically wanted to do was I have three types of ads running I wanted to know which ad paired up with this particular landing page gave me the best performance and you can see on a fairly low spend what the performance looked like and the results were quite stuck these were these created our lowest cost per acquisition that we've ever seen using the only difference were we were just changing the headlines for these particular ads and matching them to this one new landing page and the results were massive you know if you look at the the spend and the kind of performance here we were getting cost for purchases that we'd never seen before for this particular product and therefore we'd validated not only that the landing page worked because we'd already done that but we found specifically the type of ad and angle that worked best for this particular landing page as well and and the the cool thing about this is we used exactly the same audiences so we're just literally targeted fans so these are warm prospects already um and and within that we literally just split that traffic into three separate adsets and it meant that if someone landed into one particular ad set they would only ever see that ad they would not see the other ad so there's no chance of polluting the data by running a normal ad set because what happens when you have a normal ad set is people can bounce across different ad sets they can bounce across different ads so if you're running a standard ad set and you have three ads running and and you look at performance and you say right ad one's performing so much better i bet some of you have noticed that you polls add two or add three and all of a sudden ad one doesn't perform as well that's because when you're not running a split test and you're running standard ad sets sometimes customers react to more than one ad so they can get served more than one ad they then see that in their newsfeed but then convert on something else you then look at that ad that converted and say right this is the ad that's performing when in reality other ads are contributing with a facebook split test you can absolutely be sure that if you split the audience the audience will only ever see that one ad so in in each of these um ad sets i've only got one ad running and it's the same ad in every single one the only difference is the headline is being changed so that i can test different um headlines for the same landing page so for this particular one it gave us a great result you know we weren't looking for ROAS in particular we were just looking to see what cpa came out in

## [06:05 - 08:04]

fact all of them were okay but actually the return on aspen this was greater because the the cart value was better as well so that's to give you an idea of the types of questions you can ask this one for example we were testing for a particular product sending them straight to a webinar without sign up versus sending them to the webinar via sign up and we actually found that those going straight to the webinar were actually giving us a better cost per initial checkout but the other metrics weren't as strong so it's you know you have to start your split test by asking a specific question to get an answer now for example if we had looked at how many sales were generated then we'd need to spend a lot more than we have here so probably four or five hundred pounds to do the testing but for these you know i run multiple tests and these are just a few examples whether it's e-com whether it's info product to answer specific questions so the questions could be uh which landing page is performing best and landing pages are a great one to test because you can take two landing pages split them between the same audience and see which one gives you a high conversion rate so we did that recently again for any compliant where we took their existing page we created a separate sales landing page we went through all the kind of best practices for cro and we introduced some upsells on the actual lander as in bundles and buying more and not only did we increase conversion rate we also increased the average order value and we proved that page through testing we saw an uplift in conversion rate and we saw an uplift in average order value so if you have specific tests and there's a link below to see all the variables you can push into a split test run it as a split test to give you a higher chance of that data being valid than running it outside of a split test
